chunks.text: Warehouse work is unglamorous but essential to society.
The storage of goods, sorting, and delivery.
Without these, modern distribution does not work.
Having dropped out of high school
I began working at the latest Amazon warehouse at twenty - two.
At first it was simple manual labor, but gradually I learned the work.
Warehouse work has technical aspects too.
Working alongside robots.
Barcode scanners, picking systems, conveyors.
I learned these material handling technologies.
From seven in the morning until six in the evening
I walked around until my feet seemed to sink.
Sometimes my knee hurt.
At lunchtime I would eat my bento box on my lap and read messages from my lover.
But I was proud of this work.
Whatever goods we delivered, someone was waiting.
Someone's birthday gift, someone's urgent tool for work.
After five years of work, I got a promotion to team leader.
I was an analyst of my subordinates' journal records and proposed improvements for efficiency.
But the warehouse industry is now in the middle of great change.
Automation, AI, robots.
Simple labor is disappearing.
My intention is to continue studying at night school to keep up with this change.
I am not a university graduate
but the experience I have gained on the floor is precious.
I want to build a society where people are not judged by academic background alone.
A society in which whoever works sincerely is properly rewarded.
On my resume, my warehouse experience is written with pride.
It was my starting point and the work that has supported my life.
